title: Inventory EMIS teacher-quality, SDI survey, learning-outcome, and deployment-policy data sources for low-income countries
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates national EMIS teacher-qualification data, Service Delivery Indicator surveys, learning-outcome assessments (SACMEQ, PASEC, ASER), and deployment-policy documentation into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit EMIS data-quality and cross-country comparability assessment.
inputs: National EMIS documentation and data-quality assessments for target countries
Service Delivery Indicator survey methodology and country coverage
SACMEQ, PASEC, and ASER learning-outcome assessment documentation
Published teacher-deployment policy analyses for target countries
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, geographic grain, and documented limitations — particularly EMIS data quality, qualification-definition comparability, and SDI country coverage.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering EMIS data quality, qualification-definition comparability, and cross-country harmonization.

title: Map sub-national teacher-qualification distribution for at least two low-income countries using EMIS and SDI data
outcome: A sub-national teacher-qualification distribution analysis for at least two low-income countries, documenting EMIS data quality, qualification-definition methodology, and rural-urban distribution patterns.
inputs: National EMIS teacher-qualification data for target countries
Administrative boundary data for district-level aggregation
expected_artifact: validation.md update with distribution methodology and data-quality assessment.
validation_method: Replicator confirms EMIS data extraction, qualification-definition documentation, and rural-urban comparison methodology for each target country.
done_condition: Teacher-distribution analysis includes methodology, EMIS data-quality assessment, qualification-definition documentation, and replication status for at least two countries.

title: Analyze deployment equity by linking teacher-qualification distribution to learning outcomes at district scale
outcome: A deployment-equity analysis linking teacher-qualification distribution to learning-outcome data at district scale, with explicit qualification-proxy limitations and confound controls for school resources, student background, and community wealth.
inputs: learning-outcome assessment data (SACMEQ, PASEC, ASER) where available
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with equity methodology and proxy-limitation documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms equity-linkage methodology, qualification-proxy limitations, and confound-control approach for each target country.
done_condition: Deployment-equity analysis includes methodology, qualification-proxy limitations, confound controls, and replication status.

title: Red-team qualification-proxy overstatement and punitive-transfer risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how qualification-based deployment maps could overstate teaching-quality gaps by treating qualification as equivalent to effectiveness, how results could be used for punitive teacher transfers without addressing underlying incentive structures, and how absence data could stigmatize rural teachers.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every deployment-equity claim has qualification-proxy limitations, confound controls, and explicit non-punitive-transfer disclaimers. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every deployment-equity claim is either blocked, softened to proxy-limited language with explicit qualification-effectiveness caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match education ministry and teacher-training institution decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the deployment-equity analysis would change any real decision for education ministries, teacher-training institutions, or rural-incentive program designers without creating perverse incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for education ministry and training-institution audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that qualification-based maps drive punitive transfers or that absence data stigmatizes rural teachers without addressing incentive structures. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., Kenya Ministry of Education teacher-deployment director), named decision (e.g., rural hardship allowance targeting), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for punitive teacher transfers, not for stigmatizing rural teachers), and replicated field-reality review.
